label: no
feedback: I am unsure about whether meta-rule is consistent with original intent of the writers, so I was unsure. But specific rules seem to add new meanings into the original rule. For example, "ensuring individuals remain free from asset seizure" goes beyond or is more specific than general protection of property rights. Protection of property rights could mean that AI models shouldn't assist with users' attempts to break into another person's property. But "asset seizure" reminds me of the government's search and seizure of private property. How could AI models protect unlawful seizure of the government? For Rule 3, I am not really sure why procedural fairness matters more than substantive fairness.
